| | **Biden sleeps at the wheel while Americans face an increasingly uncertain economic future:**
* The collapse of Silicon Valley Bank last week was the [largest ](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdUE1PhDAQ_TVwK4Fpl48Dh9V1D56MMWq8kNIO0AiFLUVcf73DZtXEpJlm3ryZefOwTNKsSHPIEx7qsogTmenQlBADjzmIJOcc8oiLWinALJe5TGIsAhEP0vRR4xD1OEw96haj0bVhVxYpZnEDsoF6hymIFEW6q-tGqKwWstBhX3beT3PA9wEc6a3rGslPM86RGgfKt930xVtIEgq-Q9ZL1-LsWS3tO3OLZcayzsx-dOeAHxc_VANqswwBP-AmLYB0A-dxcQoJrHUupcjljlnVV5Ikm-Z8JSlKpWkt0barE84G2crKWFVZXOfKjYvVy_TDHq1H64lMk9jeP6vTU7d7uaFyZXujqfAP7Iymaw_D4-uDehMfoSv1tJq-N3OakZPtxUq6PbzKKoOMGuHSBnDxCf7037-cBPty9m6rwS1Ff57wl3idEV1dgCA7fAOTs5iJ)bank run in modern history. It's the largest bank failure since 2008 when customers at Washington Mutual withdrew [$16.7 billion over ten days](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdUMFunDAQ_RpzM8JjFuwDh6SbHHJq0kOrXpCxZ8EqGGpM6PbrO6y2rRTJGmvePI_fe9iIqtaVAiVk5hpdCFO7zDdQgCwklEJJCSqXZWctYK2MMqJAzcpiMn7MLxHRzdMyousxn2OfDY12VmuEkzG0rOx0J-VJua4U-qLhpCAbmyGlZWXygcEznX3fc_PLz2tu54n642-6iqMIQSUNyEcTe1wT70z4weMWuA988Gua45XJ5y1N7YTObxOTZzykMagOcJ23aJHAziljSmVOPNixNSTZX653kqXW-D4Q7XAtJJ9Mb1ofbBtwX9s4b8Fty1_2HBKGRGTaxF_fvugtBRxIbdWG0TsafAAH78jteXr79tl-L9-z2Lhl9-Po16qmJPtblOQ9u8tqWP3IAG7PAG45wX_9L19_lvx3DE_HDD5RTdcF_xHvO_J7CsDq8x_kUJgz). Last week’s run on SVB, however, totaled [$42 billion in a single day.](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdUMFOhDAQ_ZpyK6EtS8uBg7ruQS_GxNV4IYUO0AgFSxHXr3fYrJqYNNPMm9fXNw8Klsk8U1wxEZkiT5iWJrIFT7hIBE-ZEoKrWKRVXXOQSivNEshJmgza9nHjAcw4TD2YFuLRt1FXSJErJZK8EpWGtKp2UmZcNCidZkxmLOqLLoRpJuKK8AOedV1j_WnHOa7HAfvtb7ySrTCGJXRAe-1bmAOttHujfnHUOtrZOYz-RMRhCUM5gLHLQMQeNmuEZxs4j4uvAcHKKK1TpXfU1X2p0bJtThdSja22rUPatjUTdNCtLq2rSwfrXPpxcWaZftijC-ACklGJHp_FfL8_QvOE49L11uDgH9hZg9vuh8eXh_o1_Yh8YabV9r2dM4lJtucocffoYqsg8ppwfn7G-Tkn_uf_7vk9pV_e3W4zfoM1nCb4JV404ksKnMj9N4sllxg)
* As [inflation continues to outpace wage growth](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdUU1v3CAQ_TX2DcsevDZ78CHpJoceqqqXVLlYGGa9qHw4gONufn3Hq00rVUIjeO_NY3jg0HT9sRMgGl7q4Vg3stelGaAGXnNoG8E5iIq3k1KAvZBCNjUei7Z20tjqHBF1cItFPWMV4lxeBjUdJhCCDKaWy05IRNED1hMoODS9KO1wyXlJBX8o4JnWtm3VOfye1mQ8plSp4AhdgjXZqETbyWj0bJHG58RiSFe2GJXXiMz4s5XZBGIjte6QCj6tDmNiyUlr2actC5vf0YWKSZmuf16zGx1qs7qCn3B_UAHdDqawRoUETlpI2Qp5YF7ZUdJDzfl6Fyk6SjN7ku1ZNZw5OcvReDV63NIYw-r1unyqg8_oM4nJiT2-vH57P8zy4xfRo7dGE_EfeDGahjy5Hz-_q9f2vYyDXjZjrUldT_nPtw-grMr7WEPRPxYAtzaAW7rwb_6vL28t-4j-aefgC9V8XfCv8O5R3VOAoj_9AQX3sWk), the total amount of credit card debt held by Americans is nearing a historic high, up [18.5% from the previous year](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdkE1r3DAQhn-NfZOxPnYtH3xIu8khhVJKSksvZizJtog-XFmu2fz6jpdtCwEx0rzzajR6TEfPTXuWTFJe6q6tKTS6tB2rGa85E1RyzmTFxaAUM40ECbQ2bSFqD9ZVYzJGR784oydTxTSVcydUM0rR1nAaToZSJuV4ki3QVjQDHwWUrptzXtaCPxTsCde-75UKg6pU9JgeT-NW30KLAVaiktE2EwVJE22GTGabVxLMjodpJnPcVjNHpw8NEgEyJAOvNkxkiTbkas7eFfxpy7732GnzBb-Y4wcFOx_iGrekDIqDlgBCwokE5XrAn9nxejcpTMFOAW0HHMqJhwl6G1SPk6x9ilvQ2_LXHUM2IaMZOxEtv4mXK3_-9BnLfXBWY-GdOFuNUC7-648v6qf4XaZOL7t1zq7nBoFPN-LIqLyP1RXNh4Kx2zXGbjjZ__mfv_8S5C2Fx6PGPmLM18X8M957VHcKrGgufwDiOaVH) to a record $930.6 billion in 2022.
* More than ever, the United States needs a firm hand on the wheel of our economy. Joe Biden spent last week instead touting his disastrous, job-killing, growth-shrinking budget.
* Biden’s proposed budget would increase [taxes by $5.5 trillion](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdkMuO3CAQRb8G77Bs8INeeDFJz0Sa1SSKlMfGwlC4kQw4gON0vj7VHScjjYR43LpVVB0Y6q4_dYKJmhd6OFW17HVhB1YxXnHW1IJzJkreTEox6IUUsq7gRJrKSbuUJgLo4NYF9AxliHNxGUzbtiBNW4tGTKzmplVGSWg570EK3hbLcMl5TYQ_EPaEa9_30oRf05ash5RKFRyqoIIP7oq3HDe3UoiS_tVsyjSHqC6Q6GQ1eDw2_D_TNYY1JLlQY5PCQ-YYlM1XuoeISYcrAXjCn7bsRgfabo7wM9zGIay7iSlsUQGKkxZSNkK21KtllDimNdfDpPAp7YyFzjdSNadOznK0Xo0e9jTGsHm9rf_cwWfwGc1YiabPTf_47aP5gNN3o1-sxsAb8WI1Ejq7T19f1PfmZxEHve52WWzqeqQ_3_EjqeJoayD9O8LYPY2xO1v22v_zlx8N_R394y3G3uOeryv8Nx41yoMCI_35D8vIr1Y) and end domestic oil and gas production incentives. Meanwhile, Biden proposed that the[ EPA receive $2 billion](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdUE1PhDAU_DXlBoG2S-HAQcU9eDIas8YL6ccDmpSWBbq4_nrLBjUxaZp23rz3ZgaqLGdlXuAiI5GqyjTjTEW6wikmKcE0KwjBRUKokBIDK3jBsxRKRNOBa5O0E4Byw2hAdZC4qYv6SuQlKQ95S2hGSclYm6o2p-WB0ZLnWIjIVP2yjDMidwgfw1nXNWndp4V1TqQbAjI6oxct5_DsnZ8hnmD0wmjJ7Rxf3AKx0ApsLHxYuyBy9MvQDKC0HxCpYZOGcL6Bs_OThAAKVXBOC36IrTQND5J1e91JMny57mygba4zEg-84422stlENZPzVvnxh-3sAjZsrcOk-PRKz8R3b6dzKDfWaBUK_8Beq-C2Hl7en-UHvURTpcZVG6PnnIUku1uUwXm0y6oQu0cY39owvuWE__Q_nc40_prs41bDD-FeriP8EvcZyZ4CRqz-BkkDmuQ) for “advancing racial equity and securing environmental justice.”
* Economist Stephen Moore [described](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxVUMuO3CAQ_Br7hmUD48fBhySTPeS02ktWuVgYGg-SeQTweidfv-2JkygSounq6qK7YGzabmh72jesVONQN6JTpRlpTVnNKG96xmhfMT5LSaHrRS-aGoaC11aYtdIRQHkbVlALVD4u5Q01LnTQnGtddxwTqWs2DPoiWVvPinflOt5yDqlgnwr6hGff90r793lLxkFKlfQWUZDeeXvHV46bDQSiIL8xkzLJPsobJDIbBQ7Dhv9nEqIPPomVaJMkBpGjlybfye4jNp2sBOAK9rRlO1lQZrMFu8KxTkHbA0x-ixIQnFUvBO_FhTi5TgLXNPp-kiSmwiwodD2cahixYhGTcXJysKcp-s2pLfxhe5fBZSSjEoGXne7LWwoMy5NbjTom-B-8GYUOXe3L67P8wd_KOKqwm3U1qe3Q_eVhPzpVnmONRfe5oPTRRunDW_pv_m_ff3LyK7qvR41-wTvfA_wlnhrV6QItuusHY5uwDA) Biden’s budget as a “fiscal atrocity” that would be an economic “killer.” A report from the nonpartisan Tax Foundation found that Biden’s proposed corporate tax rate hike specifically would strangle our economy and [slash nearly 140,000 jobs](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxVUMuO1DAQ_Brn5shuhzwOOSzM7oET4rKIS-RHT-Ld2M4mDmH5ejqjAYRkt-Xq6lZVYS_rpqtbaKUqXN8JqRtX-B4EKKGgkq1S0JaqMtYCNq1utRTYsUoE7efyuiK6FJYZ3YhlWsdi6rHSSPdau642ppa1M-iuHUgjtRAgirmfcl42ph4YPNE5jqM89Db5OOYUsw-4lTYF6kQ8NnpOMfQEvVKVgorxDuPGbVqXtOqMPOuffPKvSNA-O_7q55lL1Qoh-EsyGz8nmXracxgCOr8Hpi54WmBQn-CW9tUigca1Wlet_sCjnQdN1vz1_U6y9NV-jEQ705GKBz3qwUc7nEqHNe3R7csfNnnBmIlMmziEZwUqmvGN2kOcvTu3_A9O3lEql_D12xf7vfpRrL1bDnLit7qhxMdb5JRMcZfVs-YjA7iNAdzyhH_6Pz-_VfzXGh_PHnyimt8X_Eu87yjvKQBrLr8BHkOmNw).

| | **Cartels continue to threaten Americans. Only President Trump has a plan to take them head on:**
* The four Americans kidnapped in Mexico last week are not an outlier. There are[ currently](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxdUMtOwzAQ_Jrklih-NHYPOQClByohygXEJXLtbWoptoOdEMrXs6kKSEjWrLwzu5odaEgt1rWkkrDcNOuKKGFy29CKsopRTiRjVJaMH7SmIKSSilSwznjllO3LYwQwwQ09mA7KELv81EjghKhqzflKUaZXUhMhwAhSmxWrGcn75jSOQ8rYTUa3-OZ5LmeVTtZ3Y_BDSGOpg1uIEHuDdTGDpVqAVAjOpoTqQjmIViufCgefVodlH9tOo2sdGDu5jG1g8ZnRemmmMEUN2DwYqRSXalV43bcK_dvj-SrS-FW28yhbIiCscKpTrfW69TCnNobJm2n4UQc_gh9RjJuK874-mLh73O2Rbn1vDRL_midr8PSNe3590m_8I4-NGWbb9zbVAmPtLrni_fnVVpOJ24zSyxill9Don_-Hl3defEV_v3D0DnE8D_ArvO4orynQTGy-AbmdnEk) more than 550 Americans missing in Mexico.
* The Mexican drug cartels have [made](http://email.mail.freedompledge.org/c/eJxVUMuO1DAQ_Brn5ihuZ_I45ADMLogTQkggLpFjdzK9m9jBdia7fD3OaGCFZFmq6uru6sJOVHVbNdAImZmuLYSqTUYdFCALCaVopIQml-WgNWDdqEaJAltWFouiOR89onHLOqOZMHd-yi7dAK0wpRiFSR16KNsWzWmAocASKtOIbO4uMa6ByXcMHtPb9z3fA21Hf4LHal5ILiCBiC8qcDeOpEnNgV_UFXl0s-EeAxm0MfAFX0g7TiEVHDfKTujddiB-pUCRj87zsHqyEx88qmcmH7e49Asa2hYmz3jcwqA6yOA2rzGRg2mUKht14lbPvUo30vh6F-kEFU02yY6YhOSLmlRPVvcW99Cn9dZs61-1szEZTeI0ieMUPn769nSKz6nc25nM4eB_8kImxXNevv74on-W18x3Zt1pnilUdYp-umWv3ZLdbXWsfs8Abm0At2Dhzf_n779K_tvbh6MGH9IfX1f8J7zPyO8pAKvPfwAZwqx4) Mexico unsafe for Americans to visit. When the cartels are not busy terrorizing their own country, they are producing and smuggling deadly drugs into the U.S. that destroy communities.
* The cartels must be defeated. name: Kimberly Guilfoyle
subject: It’s been a while, Peter.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
| |
---
| | | Hey, Kimberly Guilfoyle here. _It 's been a while!_

The last time we talked, we were in the heat of a battle to win back the House and fire Nancy Pelosi once and for all.

**GOOD NEWS:** we won! We officially have a Republican Speaker of the House.

However, our fight wasn't finished with reclaiming the Majority. Did you hear that House Democrats outraised Republicans 2 to 1 in January? That's no joke.

It means Democrats were raking in hundreds of thousands of dollars a DAY for an entire month... And they'll continue surging ahead unless we decide right now to correct course. **On top of that, we 're on a tight timeline because our FIRST major fundraising deadline of the year is closing in on us and we need to move NOW.**

Why does any of this matter? Fundraising is the name of the game when it comes to winning or losing elections. The amount of money will directly affect the quality of staff you can hire, how many ads you can run, how many doors you can knock, etc.

Missing fundraising deadlines means slashing budgets for key voter contact operations when they matter most.

_**[Can I personally ask you for financial support to help us catch up to the Democrats and ELIMINATE their fundraising lead?](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62478353:JHrRDZGeN:m:1:3391001263:2E8E31F4267C7D1775EEDB264CCFA33C:r "Can I personally ask you for your
financial support to help us catch up to the Democrats and ELIMINATE their fundraising lead? $25, $10, or even just $5 is all it takes when all of us
work together when time is short. ")**_
---
| | | [STAND WITH US](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62478353:JHrRDZGeN:m:1:3391001263:2E8E31F4267C7D1775EEDB264CCFA33C:r "button")
---
Don't forget:

Your hard work and dedication delivered us the House Majority. Together, we fired Nancy Pelosi. Together, we are launching badly needed investigations. And together, we are holding Joe Biden accountable.

Now our first - and biggest - deadline is here and we need your help to catch up.

**It 's been great getting caught up, Peter, I look forward to hearing from you soon!**

For America,
Kimberly Guilfoyle
---
| | [](https://email.vervemail.com/ct/62478353:JHrRDZGeN:m:1:3391001263:2E8E31F4267C7D1775EEDB264CCFA33C:r "photo")
---

Since endorsing a commonsense abortion rights ballot initiative, we've been working around the clock to put a plan together to collect the signatures needed from every corner of Ohio.

Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. ****_INDems Oppose Flurry of Bills Attacking the LGBTQ+ Community_ This week, INDems called out the Republican supermajority at the Statehouse for its hateful agenda against Hoosier families, students and educators. In fact, Republicans in the Senate[ expanded](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671254/397881829/-575959326?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) the scope of the bill to be more harmful to LGBTQ+ Hoosiers. **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671255/397881830/-1037411463?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535)****[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671256/397881831/-1037411463?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535)** Groups from around the state are united against the IN GOP’s [‘slate of hate’](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671257/397881832/-310249991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535). Just this week, [more than 100](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671258/397881833/110331635?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) teachers from across the state signed a letter opposing these harmful and regressive bills. On Monday, dozens of students at Indianapolis Public Schools [walked out of class](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671259/397881834/1794199136?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) in protest of how the bill targets them and their friends. The differences between Statehouse Democrats and Republicans could not be more stark: while Republicans work to advance their “slate of hate”, Democrats are focused on [reducing gun crimes](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671260/397881835/-1282205249?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535), [supporting first responders](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671261/397881836/-358225170?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) and protecting [drinking water in daycares and preschools.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671262/397881837/106923430?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) See INDems’ release by [clicking here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671263/397881838/820524587?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535). ****_New law forced through by Indiana Republicans could raise your energy bill_ A [new law](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671264/397881839/338110148?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) signed by Governor Holcomb on Wednesday threatens to raise your energy bills even further. It [aims to overturn](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671265/397881840/-827758466?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) the State Supreme Court’s ruling that energy companies could not retroactively raise rates due to past pollution violations. This allows them to pass the costs onto hard-working Hoosiers when they break state and federal environmental regulations. **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671266/397881841/670317469?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535)****[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671267/397881842/670317469?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535)** Why is the Indiana Republican Party siding with energy companies against working Hoosier families? In addition to this bill, the high-tax INGOP continues to refuse to take action on the state gas tax that is already higher than surrounding states and [due to rise again](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60671268/397881843/-1313361540?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI0YWUyZS03M2NhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1eeKgqBTtWQT4HJlDX7vH-lmQX_NwXk_GDMDh8H0tCc=&emci=5c0f5a68-66ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=3b24ae2e-73ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=4925535) in April. **** Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. **INDems Oppose Flurry of Bills Attacking the LGBTQ+ Community This week, INDems called out the Republican supermajority at the Statehouse for its hateful agenda against Hoosier families, students and educators. In fact, Republicans in the Senate the scope of the bill to be more harmful to LGBTQ+ Hoosiers. Groups from around the state are united against the IN GOP’s . Just this week, teachers from across the state signed a letter opposing these harmful and regressive bills. On Monday, dozens of students at Indianapolis Public Schools in protest of how the bill targets them and their friends. The differences between Statehouse Democrats and Republicans could not be more stark: while Republicans work to advance their “slate of hate”, Democrats are focused on , and protecting See INDems’ release by . **New law forced through by Indiana Republicans could raise your energy bill A signed by Governor Holcomb on Wednesday threatens to raise your energy bills even further. It the State Supreme Court’s ruling that energy companies could not retroactively raise rates due to past pollution violations. This allows them to pass the costs onto hard-working Hoosiers when they break state and federal environmental regulations. Why is the Indiana Republican Party siding with energy companies against working Hoosier families? In addition to this bill, the high-tax INGOP continues to refuse to take action on the state gas tax that is already higher than surrounding states and in April. ** Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. This week’s bill is State Rep. Mike Andrade’s (Munster) proposal to provide bleed kits to Indiana police departments. This bill passed the House by a vote of 94-0, and by the Senate Committee on Homeland Security and Transportation on Tuesday. Rep. Andrade why he presented the bill this week, “Law enforcement up in our area and throughout the state don’t have the resources, don’t have the funding to be able to get the bleeding control kit.” You can read more about HB 1396 by . **** Marion County is MARCHing to the Polls ** The Marion County Democratic Party continues its “MARCHing to the Polls” event series this Tuesday at the Washington Township Government Center. You are invited to share your ideas about how to increase voter turnout and have the chance to meet local leaders. Visit the link below to find more details and register for the "MARCHing to the Polls" event for Washington and Lawrence Townships! 2023 IDEA Convention: Book Your Room Today! INDems IN the News CBS Chicago: IBJ: WISHTV Indianapolis: WFYI Indianapolis: South Bend Tribune: IBJ: WFYI Indianapolis: WISH TV Indianapolis: FOX 59 Indianapolis: AP: WTHR Indianapolis: WISH TV Indianapolis: Indiana Capital Chronicle: Indiana Capital Chronicle: Opportunities with INDems** To apply for a job with the Indiana Democratic Party, please visit or send a note to mailto:jobs@indems.org. name: =?utf-8?Q?Caleb=20Heimlich?=
subject: 🚨 Urgent: Help us stop the Democrats from expanding income tax in WA!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party
body:
Help stop the Democrats' progressive income tax agenda in WA! Your donation can make a difference in electing Republicans to protect our communities.

http://wsrp.org
Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WeGlgXqOx-Y

With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms.

We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! (https://form.jotform.com/212737084845058)
State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates
https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws
State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. (https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws)
Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate
Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press:

“We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions.

“Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative."

Read the full statement. (https://jtwilcox.houserepublicans.wa.gov/2021/10/18/republican-leaders-todays-vaccine-deadline-threatens-critical-services-public-safety/?fbclid=IwAR3WxI9H29hbbmfbZhdkDS9QjqdVd2TIRCdjNjUkUlLdeThtbUjK_WBWJuw)
Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter
Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here (https://www.smileyforwashington.com/issues) .
Contact:
Steve Johnson
Communications Director
Washington State Republican Party
(425) 460-0570
steve@wsrp.org (mailto:steve@wsrp.org)

He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative." Read the full statement. () Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here () . Contact: Steve Johnson Communications Director Washington State Republican Party (425) 460-0570 steve@wsrp.org (mailto:steve@wsrp.org) ============================================================

name: Team Baldwin
subject: Only a matter of time
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1001&akid=81%2E35263%2EsprA8x ]TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE

Peter — In the past few weeks, no less than six potential
Republican candidates have been floated as potential challengers to
Tammy’s Senate seat.

These candidates include former Speaker of the House Paul Ryan and former
Sheriff David Clarke Jr., a MAGA-loyalist so extreme he was banned from
Fox News. And over the coming weeks, we’re sure more names will be
considered as possible candidates.

Here’s the thing: regardless of which Republicans decide to challenge
Tammy’s seat in the U.S. Senate, we know that this race will be
competitive — and expensive.

Last year, Republican super PACs spent hundreds of millions to go after
vulnerable Democratic incumbents to try to flip the Senate. They’ll spend
big to prop up whoever ends up on the Republican ticket to challenge
Tammy.

Not only that: with a presidential election at the top of the ticket, we

name: Lisa Blunt Rochester
subject: childhood photos
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for CongressLisa Blunt Rochester for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo_logo [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo_logo] https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo_logo [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo_logo]May I be vulnerable for a moment, please?
When this photo was taken, I would not have believed that I’d be the first woman and person of color that Delaware would send to Congress.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo]
Don’t get me wrong, public service and activism are values that I grew up with.
My Dad made sure that we had a copy of the Afro American Encyclopedia so that my siblings and I could learn about the Civil Rights movement and the Black Power movement – this education is one of the things that motivated me to find my voice in the demonstrations and protests of the 1970s.
But here’s the thing: at the time, we didn’t have any formalized Black history courses, and oftentimes Black women leaders went unrecognized. Many of them still aren’t.
I’m proud to be the first woman and first woman of color to represent the First State in Congress, but I’m determined not to be the last.
Will you please make a $10 donation right now to help ensure that we continue to diversify and grow our leaders? Your donation will be put to work immediately. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo]
March is Women’s History Month and while I hope that we value the leadership of women and women of color year round, it is especially salient today.
Thank you,
Lisa
Congresswoman Lisa Blunt Rochester is a Democrat representing the First State, Delaware. She served as the National Co-Chair of the Biden-Harris campaign and Co-Chair of the DCCC ‘Red to Blue’ program. Lisa is running for reelection to continue fighting for our health care, leading the charge toward environmental justice, and advocating for working families like hers.
DONATE MONTHLY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-mar2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230324_photo_bio&refcode2=biobox&recurring=1]
Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for CongressLisa Blunt Rochester for Congress
PO BOX 9767
Wilmington, DE 19810
United States

Peter,

We’re less than two weeks away from Chicago’s critical mayoral runoff election, and we’re calling on our supporters to help get education champion Paul Vallas over the finish line.

Paul’s record of public service in school districts in Chicago and across the country means he has firsthand knowledge of the challenges that Chicago communities are facing.

[Paul sailed through the first round of voting, entering the runoff in first place, but he needs your support in these crucial final weeks. Click here to split a contribution between Paul Vallas and Democrats Serve >>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/60671852/397883240/-319513557?amount=10&source=DS_EM_FR_90DO_230324_1_1&refcode2=565e3ec6-75ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&contactdata=LtrdmJSaWUYnZekPVqIsFI6THHUZAb0Fn4WMw9TjRszJPTjMQu0tp5RaiDWR1vHdzZrrVOhYQYpFrCbDboAydv+xELwZYi/AFNGC+jap6Fg69O+fRdeB7sZuAymw2EaCZ4/FxqdSpV0CrLLWYvveKaayXCBXvLlyNXQOs6Iv5gQ=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzkwNzg3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU2NWUzZWM2LTc1Y2EtZWQxMS1hOGUwLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WAMayZKQJFjYb0ehuXxcHtd6K9G_DYjxVW3ulYDtobw=) | | [GIVE $10 NOW](https://click.everyaction.com/k/60671853/397883241/-319513557?amount=10&refcode2=565e3ec6-75ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&contactdata=LtrdmJSaWUYnZekPVqIsFI6THHUZAb0Fn4WMw9TjRszJPTjMQu0tp5RaiDWR1vHdzZrrVOhYQYpFrCbDboAydv+xELwZYi/AFNGC+jap6Fg69O+fRdeB7sZuAymw2EaCZ4/FxqdSpV0CrLLWYvveKaayXCBXvLlyNXQOs6Iv5gQ=&source=DS_EM_FR_90DO_230324_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzkwNzg3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU2NWUzZWM2LTc1Y2EtZWQxMS1hOGUwLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WAMayZKQJFjYb0ehuXxcHtd6K9G_DYjxVW3ulYDtobw=)

Democrats Serve is a national organization that backs Democrats running for office with frontline public service backgrounds, including first responders, firefighters, social workers, police officers, prosecutors, judges, public defenders, educators, and more.

Democratic candidates with professional experience serving their communities in government have a pragmatic approach to governing and know how to get things done, making for strong narratives and an ability to connect with voters uniquely.

However, when running for office, they often lack access to the professional campaign staff and wealthy donor networks that non-working class candidates do.

| | Sleep is a time for the brain and body to engage in vital growth and repair. Yet, many people aren’t getting enough of it. Only a quarter of adults get the recommended 7 to 9 hours of sleep per night. **In the short term, not enough sleep or poor sleep leads to problems with memory, concentration, mood, and daytime sleepiness. In the long term, poor sleep has been linked to a higher chance of developing diabetes, obesity, and heart disease.** To wake up feeling well-rested, we must get sufficient amounts of both slow-wave and REM sleep. We feel most refreshed when we wake up during light sleep. If you’re getting enough sleep, your body will wake up on its own - no need for an alarm clock. **A sustainable way to train your body to wake up at the right time is to keep a consistent routine, practice proper sleep hygiene, and ensure you get enough hours of sleep overall.** Learn more about how to get a good night's sleep at [SleepFoundation.org](https://trk.klclick.com/ls/click?upn=TkVdrPNxuhJ01OVeLW3Zmb7oVphrhlinOUEn7YLa7D54a5eDB4Fwu-2BvPCWeTnQ3NzXqiWKzZqRDi75ntTCvNTJBBt7jPqsxIhNYEsKCii5ciQsBmWnOtOEPQhWMotjp5D0vBhR1JLpCaK-2FACjEGSIeqUtWz7yAYevZhKmXQKLUdWM6dbxi5TjMurkUrXx-2FNsoqD1VTaWbwQH9Fq-2BMpKzywHDLbNJYyhOgJtP5v2kfoyRm1smktW7pnLMuxWqr-2BX-2FSbm5TD6GEggtHYOObjhlM-2FJLjeh7GvRsghVB-2BIsAmma7rKjowUx17Qnk2qc6fjsbHneG_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymjdyn9Meo-2BU4memdpyMXxi7PQkRxByc9P11C5B-2BdKI-2F3NS-2FjKV9YCMPvX5uL1t0Ux8EUKUBaKTT-2BpzkYGxKDbSTpr1QuA82O3IjM55x5htaPyHW7cdBc-2FpcN2a-2BwUukl0UKUyoe7ND5fu2GROYbxwEzhqaR-2FEw8Dx86NpRbUgCAtgZJEt5IsoBPeKB1ZKlzgrDXp1M512UqnQZrAC7V0FLGO6B1SZTDyeiy-2FadIeXhRK2EhyHWVPxMjtQVrPS3s5chR3XbmQr0VnL43y4VeNkkSLz11xgg8rm7JzdUr-2Bvxsu-2FGEwkr7AE9DY9IHdgKRByoNIhjlBgCaztKGAWS17Lqq).

If you do not wish to receive emails from us, . | | How To Wake Up Feeling Refreshed | | | | | | Sleep is a time for the brain and body to engage in vital growth and repair. Yet, many people aren’t getting enough of it. Only a quarter of adults get the recommended 7 to 9 hours of sleep per night. In the short term, not enough sleep or poor sleep leads to problems with memory, concentration, mood, and daytime sleepiness. In the long term, poor sleep has been linked to a higher chance of developing diabetes, obesity, and heart disease. To wake up feeling well-rested, we must get sufficient amounts of both slow-wave and REM sleep. We feel most refreshed when we wake up during light sleep. If you’re getting enough sleep, your body will wake up on its own - no need for an alarm clock. A sustainable way to train your body to wake up at the right time is to keep a consistent routine, practice proper sleep hygiene, and ensure you get enough hours of sleep overall. Learn more about how to get a good night's sleep at . | | | | | | | | | | | | Trending Topics | | | | | | Can Weighted Blankets Help You Sleep? | | | | | Foods That Help You Sleep Better | | | | | | Can Melatonin Cause Nightmares? | | | | | Why You Have Poor Quality Sleep | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Sleep Foundation, A OneCare Media Company 1414 NE 42nd St 400 Seattle, WA 98105 | | |

name: Fair Courts America
subject: Hillary’s revenge…
body:
| | | Peter, _She's baaaaaaaaaack!_ **Hillary Clinton is back on the scene, and it may surprise you what her top priority is:**_[**the Wisconsin State Supreme Court race**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62476838:6ZcOiZGvN:m:1:3331421248:20EBF792739951720D7768F98D332598:r)_**.** She's raising money for radical progressive judicial candidate Janet Protasiewicz. And it makes sense -- just like Hillary, she's a far-left, pro-abortion progressive.
---
[](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62476838:6ZcOiZGvN:m:1:3331421248:20EBF792739951720D7768F98D332598:r)
---
But plenty of state-level races have progressive candidates...why is Hillary so invested in this one? **Because there's one thing Hillary knows after her bitter loss to President Donald Trump in 2016:****Wisconsin is KEY to any national Democrat victory****.** She learned the hard way that Wisconsin NEEDS to be a part of "the blue wall" for any Democrat candidate. When the Left loses Wisconsin, they lose the White House.
---
[](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62476838:6ZcOiZGvN:m:1:3331421248:20EBF792739951720D7768F98D332598:r)
---
It's why the Left is so passionate about capturing Wisconsin's state supreme court--to make sure a loss in this purple state never happens again. _Because if the Left controls the highest court..._
* A progressive majority can rule against any laws combatting voter fraud
* A progressive majority can change the rules about how the state conducts elections
* And a progressive majority can rule on any legal challenges to the vote totals.

Hillary's preferred judicial candidate, Janet Protasiewicz, is already on record blasting "rigged" congressional maps and promising to "take a fresh look" at them if elected. **Translation: She won't believe Wisconsin elections are fair until they are rigged in favor of Democrats.**
---
[**DON 'T LET THE LEFT STEAL WISCONSIN**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62476838:6ZcOiZGvN:m:1:3331421248:20EBF792739951720D7768F98D332598:r)
---
Let's be clear: If Democrats control their state supreme court, Wisconsin will go from swing state to solidly blue..._even if they have to bus in voters from Chicago._ **_[We have to stop Hillary's revenge and make sure that Wisconsin's elections don't fall into the clutches of progressive judicial activists.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62476838:6ZcOiZGvN:m:1:3331421248:20EBF792739951720D7768F98D332598:r)_** Our organization is fighting tooth and nail to elect solid conservative Justice Daniel Kelly back to the Wisconsin Supreme Court. But we only have a few weeks left to go...and now that Hillary's directed her MASSIVE political operation to aid radical Janet Protasiewicz, we need every conservative to pitch in so we fight back. **_Can we count on you?_**
---
[DONATE NOW](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62476838:6ZcOiZGvN:m:1:3331421248:20EBF792739951720D7768F98D332598:r)
---
In Justice, Fair Courts America
---

And we were able to communicate our new vision because people like you read emails like this and invested in our movement one dollar at a time. It may sound cheesy, but it’s absolutely true. name: Madhur Bansal, EMILYs List
subject: We protected abortion access for tens of millions in 2022. Now, we can do it again →
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILYs List [www
body:
|
---
| | When choice is on the ballot, choice wins. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/60672841/397887266/402280152?sourceid=1048809&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZTYxYzMzNC03OGNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R3hvD4k-d4HLSvVhcmpmRLj7D6X9nSapeYXtlhl7qog=&emci=213da0e9-6cca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=2e61c334-78ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934) Peter,

In striking down _Roe v. Wade_ last June, the Supreme Court ruled that Americans do not have the constitutional right to an abortion. As a result, reproductive health care is being attacked and dismantled state by state — as you can see in the map below.

But it's not all bad news: _thanks to the success of Democrats in last year's midterm elections_ , we were able to **protect abortion access** for tens of millions of Americans. Now, we're gearing up to do it again in 2023 and 2024.

[**Are you ready to keep fighting back? Donate now to help elect the next wave of Democratic pro-choice women who will work to protect and restore abortion rights nationwide.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/60672865/397887320/402280152?sourceid=1048809&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZTYxYzMzNC03OGNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R3hvD4k-d4HLSvVhcmpmRLj7D6X9nSapeYXtlhl7qog=&emci=213da0e9-6cca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=2e61c334-78ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934) [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/60672867/397887322/402280152?sourceid=1048809&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZTYxYzMzNC03OGNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R3hvD4k-d4HLSvVhcmpmRLj7D6X9nSapeYXtlhl7qog=&emci=213da0e9-6cca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=2e61c334-78ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934) Here's a breakdown of where abortion laws currently stand across the country:
1. Abortion is **banned** in Idaho, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Missouri, Arkansas, Oklahoma, Texas, Louisiana, Kentucky, Tennessee, Mississippi, Alabama, Georgia, and West Virginia.

2. In Arizona, Utah, Wyoming, North Dakota, Ohio, Indiana, and South Carolina, laws banning abortion have been **temporarily blocked by courts**.

3. Abortion is still legal — **for now** — in Montana, Nebraska, Iowa, Virginia, North Carolina, and Florida. In North Carolina, the GOP-controlled legislature is working on a ban that the Democratic governor has vetoed before — and they only need the support of _one_ Democrat in the state legislature to override the veto. In the other five states, the only way to _ensure_ the continued legality of abortion is to flip their state legislatures and/or defeat their GOP governors in 2024.

4. In the 23 remaining states, abortion is legal and **likely to remain protected** — _if_ we're able to protect the Senate and take back the House in 2024. Republicans in Congress have made it clear they intend to pass a **national abortion ban** if they regain power.

The last election proved that the only way to prevent and reverse abortion bans is to keep anti-choice Republicans out of power at _all_ levels of government. We now know that when choice is on the ballot, choice wins.

clean_body: | | | When choice is on the ballot, choice wins. Peter, In striking down Roe v. Wade last June, the Supreme Court ruled that Americans do not have the constitutional right to an abortion. As a result, reproductive health care is being attacked and dismantled state by state — as you can see in the map below. But it's not all bad news: thanks to the success of Democrats in last year's midterm elections , we were able to protect abortion access for tens of millions of Americans. Now, we're gearing up to do it again in 2023 and 2024. Here's a breakdown of where abortion laws currently stand across the country: 1. Abortion is banned in Idaho, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Missouri, Arkansas, Oklahoma, Texas, Louisiana, Kentucky, Tennessee, Mississippi, Alabama, Georgia, and West Virginia. 2. In Arizona, Utah, Wyoming, North Dakota, Ohio, Indiana, and South Carolina, laws banning abortion have been temporarily blocked by courts. 3. Abortion is still legal — for now — in Montana, Nebraska, Iowa, Virginia, North Carolina, and Florida. In North Carolina, the GOP-controlled legislature is working on a ban that the Democratic governor has vetoed before — and they only need the support of one Democrat in the state legislature to override the veto. In the other five states, the only way to ensure the continued legality of abortion is to flip their state legislatures and/or defeat their GOP governors in 2024. 4. In the 23 remaining states, abortion is legal and likely to remain protected — if we're able to protect the Senate and take back the House in 2024. Republicans in Congress have made it clear they intend to pass a national abortion ban if they regain power. The last election proved that the only way to prevent and reverse abortion bans is to keep anti-choice Republicans out of power at all levels of government. We now know that when choice is on the ballot, choice wins. Donate to help elect Democratic pro-choice women who will fight for reproductive rights everywhere in 2023 and 2024: Thank you, Madhur Bansal (she/her/hers) Vice President of Research, EMILYs List | ---|---|--- EMILYs List is a community of over five million members that helps elect Democratic pro-choice women to office. | Paid for by EMILYs List and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. body:
|
#
---
Peter, This isn’t a typical political email like the other ones probably clogging your inbox. Today we’re asking you to [_**make a donation to Oregon Food Bank — a great organization working to provide food to folks facing hunger throughout our state.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60673377/397888874/1889040911?refcode=AU_TK_FR_CHA_GEN_AC_20230324_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDVjNWM0Zi03YWNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KxYYJZAvpUldjrnfvlR6ujbkO05QrKZZo1e0Ip5Pdaw=&emci=ef4f0541-43ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=205c5c4f-7aca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=15353729#!/donation/checkout) More than 62,000 Oregonians face hunger In Oregon. During the COVID-19 public health emergency, the federal government expanded food assistance programs to help people feed themselves and their families. Those benefits have ended. Now, the statewide network of food banks and local food pantries that Oregon Food Bank supports are seeing more and more people coming to them for emergency food. We ask that you do what you can today to help your neighbors. Tina will keep working on improving access to affordable housing and health care to help on her end. Meanwhile, please consider donating something to Oregon Food Bank today. Tina’s mission has always been about people working together to build a stronger, brighter future for our state. If every single person reading this email chips in just a few bucks, Oregon Food Bank will be able to multiply its impact in the months ahead. [_**This is a great way to come together as a team to make Oregon a better place to live. Please, if you’re able, consider making a contribution to the Oregon Food Bank today:**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60673380/397888877/1889040911?refcode=AU_TK_FR_CHA_GEN_AC_20230324_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDVjNWM0Zi03YWNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KxYYJZAvpUldjrnfvlR6ujbkO05QrKZZo1e0Ip5Pdaw=&emci=ef4f0541-43ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=205c5c4f-7aca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=15353729#!/donation/checkout) | [DONATE TO OREGON FOOD BANK →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60673383/397888880/1889040911?refcode=AU_TK_FR_CHA_GEN_AC_20230324_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDVjNWM0Zi03YWNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KxYYJZAvpUldjrnfvlR6ujbkO05QrKZZo1e0Ip5Pdaw=&emci=ef4f0541-43ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=205c5c4f-7aca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=15353729#!/donation/checkout)

name: Mariah Morgan
subject: Have you signed our petition?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for Congress Wiley Nickel for Congress
body:
Wiley’s been fighting to keep the redistricting maps that the Supreme Court approved last year in place. But the far right is doing everything they can to have them overturned. | | | Team - Wiley’s been fighting to protect fair maps. But the far right is doing everything they can to have them overturned. We must keep our districts fair and not give representatives the ability to choose their constituents. With the help of you and this grassroots team, we made history last November – flipping four chambers, adding three new Democratic trifectas, and defending every single majority. **But we can’t take our foot off the gas now – the stakes are too high.**
State Democrats are facing a slew of special elections over the next two months – and the Virginia House and Senate are in play this November.
**Electing Democratic leaders who will support President Biden’s agenda in statehouses across the country is _critical_ to building a better future in your state. But it’s up to grassroots supporters like you to own a piece of this fight and give state Democrats the resources they need to go toe-to-toe against their Republican opponents. **

name: PA Democrats
subject: Governor Shapiro Is Tackling the Climate Crisis
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/90471/images/PDP%20Primary%20Logo.png]
Hey Peter,
Earlier this year, we sent around a survey asking what issues mattered most to you and your communities. The top answer by far was our environment.
We couldn’t agree with you more. Pennsylvania is home to some of our nation’s greatest national treasures. From our parks to our lakes and streams, protecting our natural resources for generations to come is a top priority for Pennsylvania Democrats.
Because we know that this issue is at the top of your mind, we wanted to make sure you had a chance to hear how Governor Shapiro’s new budget addresses the climate crisis, creates thousands of green jobs, and stimulates Pennsylvania’s economy!
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pa2023?refcode=march22email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pa2023?refcode=march22email]
Here’s everything you need to know:
*
The
budget
plans
to
tackle
a
significant
source
of
greenhouse
gas
emissions
here
in
Pennsylvania,
which
would
create
thousands
of
good-paying,
union
jobs
in
the
process!
*
Governor
Shapiro’s
budget
would
invest
almost
$6
million
into
improving
air
quality
testing,
increasing
dam
inspections,
safeguarding
water
quality,
and
more.
*
The
Governor
is
also
working
to
cap
and
plug
abandoned
wells
that
are
scattered
across
rural
Pennsylvania
–
an
estimated
350,000
wells
that
make
up
nearly
8%
of
our
total
methane
emissions.
*
In
protecting
our
environment,
we
are
protecting
Pennsylvania’s
agriculture
industry
which
is
a
leading
provider
of
food
and
medicine
throughout
the
world
while
providing
jobs
throughout
the
Commonwealth.
Having leaders like Governor Shapiro who are committed to environmental justice is just one part of what makes Pennsylvania so great. Help us protect Democratic leaders like Governor Shapiro by chipping in $10 or $20 now. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pa2023?refcode=march22email]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Donate $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pa2023?refcode=march22email&express_lane=true&amount=10]
Donate $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pa2023?refcode=march22email&express_lane=true&amount=25]

Let’s keep Pennsylvania’s air and water clean.

clean_body: [] Hey Peter, Earlier this year, we sent around a survey asking what issues mattered most to you and your communities. The top answer by far was our environment. We couldn’t agree with you more. Pennsylvania is home to some of our nation’s greatest national treasures. From our parks to our lakes and streams, protecting our natural resources for generations to come is a top priority for Pennsylvania Democrats. Because we know that this issue is at the top of your mind, we wanted to make sure you had a chance to hear how Governor Shapiro’s new budget addresses the climate crisis, creates thousands of green jobs, and stimulates Pennsylvania’s economy! [] Here’s everything you need to know: The budget plans to tackle a significant source of greenhouse gas emissions here in Pennsylvania, which would create thousands of good-paying, union jobs in the process! Governor Shapiro’s budget would invest almost $6 million into improving air quality testing, increasing dam inspections, safeguarding water quality, and more. The Governor is also working to cap and plug abandoned wells that are scattered across rural Pennsylvania – an estimated 350,000 wells that make up nearly 8% of our total methane emissions. In protecting our environment, we are protecting Pennsylvania’s agriculture industry which is a leading provider of food and medicine throughout the world while providing jobs throughout the Commonwealth. Having leaders like Governor Shapiro who are committed to environmental justice is just one part of what makes Pennsylvania so great. Help us protect Democratic leaders like Governor Shapiro by chipping in $10 or $20 now. [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Donate $10 [] Donate $25 [] Donate $50 [] Donate $100 [] Donate $250 [] Other [] Let’s keep Pennsylvania’s air and water clean. Pennsylvania Democrats PA Democratic Party 229 State Street Harrisburg, PA 17101 United States []

name: =?utf-8?Q?Illinois=20Review?=
subject: ALERT: Pritzker, IL DEMs Fund $800K to Attack Candidates
body:
If there was ever a case for educational malpractice, this would be it. The Democrats continue to prove they are the extremists.

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/f172dc056fdd/alert-pritzker-il-dems-fund-800k-to-attack-candidates?e=1cb5fdae9e)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
https://www.goldentreewealth.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Pritzker, IL Democratic Party Fund $800K to Attack School Board Candidates, Including Women and Single Parents While Reading, Math Scores Plummet (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/pritzker-il-democratic-party-fund-800k-to-attack-school-board-candidates-including-women-and-single-parents-while-reading-math-scores-plummet.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Gov. JB Pritzker has made quality education one of his top priorities, even marketing the phrase, “Improving Education from Cradle to Career” – and highlighting an increase in education funding by more than $1 billion; raising the minimum teacher salary; and expanding investments in vocational and technical training in high schools across the state.

But the reality is that our schools are failing – children are falling further behind in their learning and student test scores continue to plummet. Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/pritzker-il-democratic-party-fund-800k-to-attack-school-board-candidates-including-women-and-single-parents-while-reading-math-scores-plummet.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/pritzker-il-democratic-party-fund-800k-to-attack-school-board-candidates-including-women-and-single-parents-while-reading-math-scores-plummet.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Effingham County Judge Declines to Stay DeVore's Cases Against Gov. Pritzker's Assault Weapons Ban (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/effingham-county-judge-declines-to-stay-devores-cases-against-gov-pritzkers-assault-weapons-ban.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

On Tuesday, attorneys from Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul’s office argued a motion to stay the consolidated cases challenging Gov. JB Pritzker’s Assault Weapons Ban brought by former Republican candidate for Attorney General Thomas DeVore. Effingham County Chief Judge Douglas L. Jarman declined to stay the cases and set a hearing date in May to resolve DeVore’s discovery requests. Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/effingham-county-judge-declines-to-stay-devores-cases-against-gov-pritzkers-assault-weapons-ban.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/effingham-county-judge-declines-to-stay-devores-cases-against-gov-pritzkers-assault-weapons-ban.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

U.S. Representative Mary Miller's Congressional Family Caucus (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/u-s-representative-mary-millers-congressional-family-caucus.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Earlier this month, U.S. Representative Mary Miller (R, IL-15) announced the launch of the Congressional Family Caucus from the floor of the U.S. House of Representatives. In a brief but spirited speech, she explained that the caucus’ purpose is to defend the natural family from Left-grounded erosion. She subsequently criticized the Left for its support of “abortion, fatherlessness, surgical castration, and atheism,” as well as “eliminating parental consent and replacing families with the state.” Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/u-s-representative-mary-millers-congressional-family-caucus.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/u-s-representative-mary-millers-congressional-family-caucus.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

IL GOP Leaders Rally Behind DeSantis as Trump Surges and Maintains an Impressive Double-Digit Lead over FL Governor in Latest Polls (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/il-gop-leaders-rally-behind-desantis-as-trump-surges-and-maintains-an-impressive-double-digit-lead-over-fl-governor-in-latest-polls.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Ever since DeSantis’ visit to the Chicago suburbs, he continues to drop in the polls, as his potential opponent – former President Donald Trump, surges – widening his double-digit lead over the FL governor.

According to Morning Consult, in February, right around the time of DeSantis’ visit to Illinois, Trump maintained a 16-point lead over DeSantis.

In March, just weeks after his visit, Trump widened that lead – leading DeSantis by now 24-points.

A similar poll conducted by TIPP had Trump leading DeSantis by 23-points in February, and 29-points a month later.
Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/il-gop-leaders-rally-behind-desantis-as-trump-surges-and-maintains-an-impressive-double-digit-lead-over-fl-governor-in-latest-polls.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/il-gop-leaders-rally-behind-desantis-as-trump-surges-and-maintains-an-impressive-double-digit-lead-over-fl-governor-in-latest-polls.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Di Leo: Background Checks and Changing the Subject (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/opinion-background-checks-and-changing-the-subject.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
Some politicians address challenges head-on. Others attempt distractions, changing the subject in the hope that the public will forget the ongoing disasters of their governance.

It should be no surprise by now that Joe Biden falls in the latter group.

As the American economy continues to stagnate, with high inflation, a sluggish stock market, and the promise of home ownership moving into unreachable territory, Joe Biden has returned to one of his favorite cons: the idea that he can make Americans safer by expanding unconstitutional gun control measures.
Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/opinion-background-checks-and-changing-the-subject.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/opinion-background-checks-and-changing-the-subject.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Sources: Paranoid Pekau Installs Bulletproof Glass, Frosts Windows in Orland Park Village Board Room, Making Case for Security Detail if Referendum Passes (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/sources-paranoid-pekau-installs-bulletproof-glass-frosts-windows-in-orland-park-village-board-room-making-case-for-security-detail-if-referendum-passes.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Orland Park Mayor Keith Pekau is obsessed with control – and it’s why he’s pushing a referendum that would give him virtually unlimited power in Orland Park.

He controls the Village Trustees.

He controls restaurants and bars as the liquor commissioner.

He controls who gets contracts with the Village.

And now he wants complete and total control of all Village activities and day-to-day operations under a referendum that was presented at the last minute – without any input from the community; and without a comprehensive plan, during the January 16, 2023 Village Board meeting.
Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/sources-paranoid-pekau-installs-bulletproof-glass-frosts-windows-in-orland-park-village-board-room-making-case-for-security-detail-if-referendum-passes.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=d39802e68d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_03_24_04_17&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-d39802e68d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

body:
Republican Representative Lauren Boebert is receiving praise from

name: Nicole, Political Director
subject: Here’s exactly how extremists are trying to suppress our votes:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT ACTION
body:
Here’s exactly how a voter’s plan is affected by restrictive SB 202: |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60675181/397897219/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230324_f_voterjourney_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTc2NDQ3NS03ZmNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EJlkUhyqjm5g_Qfck3YzBqeHcj4NGWtylWg6zvGqS1U=&emci=01164980-61ca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=a9764475-7fca-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=1037277)

| | Hey there, I’m Nicole from the Fair Fight Action team. Have you wondered how voter suppression laws really impact voters in states like Georgia? I’m here to explain. In the last election, a law called SB 202 made it harder for Georgians to exercise their freedom to vote. We still turned out in huge numbers, but this kind of barrier should never exist — so it's our mission to make sure these obstacles are dismantled. Before we tell you how Fair Fight Action continues to fight back against voter suppression efforts like SB 202, **here’s exactly how a voter’s plan is affected by these restrictions:** | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60675184/397897222/-972217868?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230324_f_voterjourney_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTc2NDQ3NS03ZmNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EJlkUhyqjm5g_Qfck3YzBqeHcj4NGWtylWg6zvGqS1U=)
---
* Am I registered to vote?
* _SB 202 made it more likely for voters to have their registration canceled._
* Have I requested my mail-in ballot?
* _SB 202 shortened the period to request a ballot by 109 days._
* Do I have my valid ID handy?
* _SB 202 requires specific photo ID in order to vote by mail — and over 200,000 Georgians don’t have the accepted IDs._
* Do I know where my drop box is?
* _SB 202 significantly reduced the total number of drop boxes and drop box availability._
* When am I planning to vote? Do I have time to wait in line?
* _SB 202 no longer allows volunteers to distribute water or snacks to voters while waiting in line._
* When can I vote in the runoff election?
* _SB 202 cut the runoff timeline by 5 weeks, meaning less time for voters to cast their ballots again._

clean_body: Here’s exactly how a voter’s plan is affected by restrictive SB 202: | | | Hey there, I’m Nicole from the Fair Fight Action team. Have you wondered how voter suppression laws really impact voters in states like Georgia? I’m here to explain. In the last election, a law called SB 202 made it harder for Georgians to exercise their freedom to vote. We still turned out in huge numbers, but this kind of barrier should never exist — so it's our mission to make sure these obstacles are dismantled. Before we tell you how Fair Fight Action continues to fight back against voter suppression efforts like SB 202, here’s exactly how a voter’s plan is affected by these restrictions: | = Am I registered to vote? SB 202 made it more likely for voters to have their registration canceled. Have I requested my mail-in ballot? SB 202 shortened the period to request a ballot by 109 days. Do I have my valid ID handy? SB 202 requires specific photo ID in order to vote by mail — and over 200,000 Georgians don’t have the accepted IDs. Do I know where my drop box is? SB 202 significantly reduced the total number of drop boxes and drop box availability. When am I planning to vote? Do I have time to wait in line? SB 202 no longer allows volunteers to distribute water or snacks to voters while waiting in line. When can I vote in the runoff election? SB 202 cut the runoff timeline by 5 weeks, meaning less time for voters to cast their ballots again. Check out even more of the reasons why SB 202 is a harmful law for voters Fair Fight Action is working around the clock to make voting easier, not more difficult. Together with our partners on the ground, our team is organizing activists in their communities and pressuring state lawmakers to take immediate action against dangerous anti-voter legislation. But that’s not all — we’re also building out robust voter education and training programs to guarantee strong turnout in future elections. It’s up to us to prove the anti-democratic legislators wrong, by showing up even stronger than we have before. = = Thank you for everything, The Fair Fight Action Team | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2023 Fair Fight Action - All rights reserved. body:
There are more women serving in Congress than ever before
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60675219/397897481/567597420?refcode=EM_FR_230324WhipSplitWHM_Women_KC_90DO_11_G_TESTB_p1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNjVkY2VhYi03ZmNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xFG36KKAC8bMuT0ik8ghCa7EnWd2shROirzb5L78WI4=) Happy Women’s History Month, Peter.

My name is Katherine Clark, and I proudly serve Massachusetts’ 5th district in Congress. I am also in House leadership serving as the Democratic Whip.

When I first came to Congress in 2013, there weren’t many women serving alongside me. Multiple times, I was stopped and mistaken for someone’s spouse, and I had a hard time even getting into the Chamber.

Thankfully, walking through the halls of Congress in 2023 is much different. **There are more women serving in Congress than ever before — 94 women in the House Democratic Caucus alone!**

I’ve been adamant about helping elect more women to Congress, because the truth is that for generations, we’ve failed to address the persistent challenges faced by women, especially by women of color.

**And with MAGA extremists in the House majority threatening to take us back even further, the stakes of our work couldn’t be higher.**

[**_That’s why I’m writing to you today, Peter. Effective leadership isn’t about individual ambition. It’s about our collective good.

Lifting up others is a core value of mine — I strongly believe that when we elect more women to positions of power, we all do better.

**Together, Shontel and I will continue the fight for gender equity and women’s rights, and with your support, elect even more candidates who are committed to building the kind of country and future that our daughters deserve.**

[**_So please, if you can afford to, split a contribution today between my campaign and Shontel Brown's and help us fulfill the country’s promise of equality and opportunity for all._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/60675254/397897543/567597420?refcode=EM_FR_230324WhipSplitWHM_Women_KC_90DO_11_G_TESTB_l2&amount=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNjVkY2VhYi03ZmNhLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xFG36KKAC8bMuT0ik8ghCa7EnWd2shROirzb5L78WI4=)

Thank you for your support, Peter! I hope you take some time this month to reflect on our achievements, celebrate the women in your life, and recommit to building a stronger, more inclusive America.

All my best,

— Katherine

It’s about our collective good. Lifting up others is a core value of mine — I strongly believe that when we elect more women to positions of power, we all do better. Together, Shontel and I will continue the fight for gender equity and women’s rights, and with your support, elect even more candidates who are committed to building the kind of country and future that our daughters deserve. = Thank you for your support, Peter! I hope you take some time this month to reflect on our achievements, celebrate the women in your life, and recommit to building a stronger, more inclusive America. All my best, — Katherine Representative Katherine Clark Massachusetts 5th Congressional District Democratic Whip = Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress Shontel Brown for Congress PO Box 221232 Beachwood, OH 44122 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

Good afternoon, friends, advocates and voters.
We hope this edition of the New Pennsylvania Project eBlast finds you well as we close out Women’s History Month.
Please take a few moments to check out all the important news you can use, events happening in your neighborhood, career opportunities, and other updates.
Graphic depicting: 2023 Municipal Primary Important Dates. May 1- Last day to register to vote for the primary election. May 9 - Last day to apply for a mail ballot. May 16 - Voted mail ballots must be received by 8pm. May 16 - Election Day. Polls are open from 7am to 8pm. vote.pa.gov [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/Image_1.jpeg]
We're Hiring!
The New Pennsylvania Project is a voting rights organization with a primary focus of registering communities of color and the youth to vote. We are looking for passionate individuals to join our team and help defend democracy by expanding the electorate.

News You Can Use
Pennsylvania judge dismisses latest GOP mail ballot lawsuit [https://apnews.com/article/pennsylvania-election-ballot-mail-vote-trump-3d7829e4fe74359da14f66524aa773da] (AP)
A commonwealth judge yesterday dismissed a lawsuit filed by the Republican National Committee that had sought to prevent counties from helping voters ensure their ballots count by fixing minor, technical deficiencies on mail-in ballot envelopes.
Politics Ups and Downs [https://www.politicspa.com/ups-and-downs-14/119540/] (PoliticsPA)
“It’s the first official week of spring and budget hearings are blooming all over the Capital City. But other news was made outside the halls of the Capitol ... providing us with many of our Ups and Downs for the week ending March 23.” [https://www.politicspa.com/ups-and-downs-14/119540/]
Tweet of the Week
The Independent State Legislature Theory that the Supreme Court is considering in Moore v. Harper is dangerous. It would push our country away from democracy and towards more extreme partisanship.
Graphic depicting tweet from Common Cause PA. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/Image.jpeg]
Policy Corner
Pennsylvania’s fiscal year begins July 1 and ends June 30 the following year. To ensure that funding for all state programs continues, the budget should be completed and signed by the Governor by midnight on June 30.
Pennsylvania Budget Process and Timeline:
1.
October:
State
agencies
submit
their
budget
requests
to
the
Governor’s
budget
office.
2.
October
-
January:
The
Governor’s
budget
office
and
other
key
staff
develop
a
budget
proposal.
3.
Beginning
of
February/March:
Governor
addresses
the
PA
General
Assembly
with
their
budget
speech
releasing
the
budget
proposal
that
highlights
aspects
of
their
proposed
budget
along
with
priorities
for
the
Commonwealth
for
the
next
fiscal
year.
4.
End
of
February
-
March:
The
PA
House
of
Representatives
and
Senate
hold
several
weeks
of
appropriations
hearings
with
all
state
agencies
about
the
details
of
their
agency’s
proposed
budget.
5.
April/May:
A
budget
is
drafted
and
introduced
as
a
bill
by
the
PA
General
Assembly
and
goes
through
the
normal
legislative
process.
After the General Assembly passes the budget bill, the Governor has the opportunity to sign the bill, veto the bill, permit the bill to become law without signing it, or line-item veto particular items.
NPPEF Graphic stating: ''#Policy Corner. A Budget is a moral document. The state budget provides insight on the state government's priorities as it determines how much is spent, or not spent, on key programs and services. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/NPPEF%20324%20-%20State%20Budget.png]
Ramadan Mubarak to all who celebrate! Wishing you a meaningful and peaceful fast.
Graphic depicting: Ramadan Kareem [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/Image_2.jpeg]
Thank you for taking the time to stay informed. We'll be back in two weeks with the latest edition.
In solidarity,
Kadida and #TeamNPP

Municipal/Local/Judicial Primary: May 16, 2023

We're on a mission to register new voters in the Commonwealth and expand the electorate.
Help us prepare for the upcoming municipal, judicial, and local elections in 2023!

**How about we start our own American version, where we advocate for freedom, individual rights, limited government, capitalism, family-first values ... maybe, we call it: **

| | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. | | Abortion is (yet again) likely to be the top issue on 2024 ballots. This time though, Peter, in _Ohio_ : [](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yscVJVvN-r4bukDGstILwI862NHve7ljnSJgtQ9E-0UUz87lspwCWF4m_mDw7dBYfJOFdzi3dywxqMDh-BZay8VQQ7Ob1ei49DMsq1DZYEe3wWSTO5pNphBfS6Jz4N7zWcKw9OQaCoF_u2-bxasslzk61QncpLtwA2fZoUSNZ2FYrellYqv2kCVSFbC5YdABze7ihm8bMVZygV1Qscwg61x7oRbjVlLbsK33YFAP-oALjvExzWCcrqgE9PG0jrtzBbMyLJB3bFClU_w9n7Q0PtywTKRLQAQnAyLXZ_ltrhQV_1pZb3mUQ2Kfis8dc2EBIvgSePu1aIMnZjrWCH7O9Q3gyMucEftJ6_JBuV_kr6X9EpgEOIEzAsmOGGzGd9PxbjFMXe6u8KlwtD-0Z2QSVyCXJTqAC_ECD7K_0tY3JomA/3ur/qhd76GQFSXCbov1515sXeA/h1/PIpceB57Wpf-HJTEP0vkagtiafe10H7t1LwEiW8vh4I) FIRST: The Supreme Court overturned _Roe v. Wade_ on June 24th, 2022, taking away the Federal Constitutional Right to Abortion.

THEN: Voters showed up and showed out in unexpected states (Kansas and Kentucky) to **vote down** constitutional amendments that would have eliminated all protections for abortion rights in the state’s constitution.

**NOW: It’s time to take the same fight to Ohio.**

Here at The Collective PAC, we know how important it is to elect pro-choice champions to Congress, **_especially Black women_** , who will take action to protect reproductive rights on day one — just like Emilia Sykes and Shontel Brown, who we helped to elect!

[And, Peter, we’re ready to elect even more progressive Black democrats in 2024. Chip in $10 now to help us go bigger and bolder so we can support representatives in Ohio and the states that will (inevitably) come after in our fight to protect abortion once and for all >>>](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yscVJVvN-r4bukDGstILwI862NHve7ljnSJgtQ9E-0UUz87lspwCWF4m_mDw7dBYfJOFdzi3dywxqMDh-BZay8VQQ7Ob1ei49DMsq1DZYEe3wWSTO5pNphBfS6Jz4N7zWcKw9OQaCoF_u2-bxasslzk61QncpLtwA2fZoUSNZ2FdHhLv6RguK55cgrs2zZ33-ZyQnyuFmZppIkn9r2TA7o2VlMbU3v1jc0rpOh6VxBq37DgVexsRj02-0_BPbChuvRYFArLtufuzf3cEzGCIczZwtr2MJyes4h7WLrNMLsk5I-1iHHby1a_HvbF26zO4pKwmesW7cuBE7IF3HXn68cTbs0a9zlENKJKNXx1EQ9LVRakDcYJzYCttwv69sLtmRwckeQwcs2jSGSHmOo68NIZlbLeP30KrDvo2Qk5ScH3A/3ur/qhd76GQFSXCbov1515sXeA/h2/trSxFZCh9PLMoz_PFDtkTVca8vlXUQtV0Do0Yyv5c-c) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.
